verses.text: David pleads God's faithfulness and righteousness, but asks Him not to 'enter into judgment,' for 'in thy sight shall no man living be justified.' He casts himself on mercy. No one can stand on his own merit before God's perfect bar.

David pleads God's faithfulness and righteousness, but asks Him not to 'enter into judgment,' for 'in thy sight shall no man living be justified.' He casts himself on mercy. No one can stand on his own merit before God's perfect bar.
The enemy has crushed him to the ground, his spirit overwhelmed and his heart desolate. He is at his lowest. There are valleys where the soul feels utterly forsaken and dark.
The enemy has crushed him to the ground, his spirit overwhelmed and his heart desolate. He is at his lowest. There are valleys where the soul feels utterly forsaken and dark.
He remembers the days of old and God's works, stretching out his hands and thirsting after Him 'as a thirsty land.' Memory feeds longing. Recalling past mercies kindles a deeper thirst for God.
He remembers the days of old and God's works, stretching out his hands and thirsting after Him 'as a thirsty land.' Memory feeds longing. Recalling past mercies kindles a deeper thirst for God.

He begs God to answer speedily and to 'cause me to hear thy lovingkindness in the morning,' showing him the way to walk. He needs both comfort and guidance. The soul that trusts God asks each morning for His love and direction.

He begs God to answer speedily and to 'cause me to hear thy lovingkindness in the morning,' showing him the way to walk. He needs both comfort and guidance. The soul that trusts God asks each morning for His love and direction.

He asks to be delivered and 'teach me to do thy will,' led by God's good Spirit 'into the land of uprightness.' He longs to obey, not merely escape. The Spirit who guides leads the believer in level paths of righteousness.

He asks to be delivered and 'teach me to do thy will,' led by God's good Spirit 'into the land of uprightness.' He longs to obey, not merely escape. The Spirit who guides leads the believer in level paths of righteousness.

'Quicken me, O LORD, for thy name's sake,' he prays, resting his plea on being God's servant. He asks revival for God's honour. The believer's life and rescue are sought for the glory of the God he serves.

'Quicken me, O LORD, for thy name's sake,' he prays, resting his plea on being God's servant. He asks revival for God's honour. The believer's life and rescue are sought for the glory of the God he serves.
